auth.usage_instructions: OAuth 1.0a authentication for the X API. Requires consumer key/secret and access token/secret from the X Developer Portal. Use login to configure, status to verify, and logout to clear.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.
login.usage_instructions: Set up X API OAuth credentials. Pass all four tokens directly with --consumer-key/-k, --consumer-secret/-s, --access-token/-t, --access-token-secret/-a, or run without flags to be prompted. Use --force/-F to clear existing credentials and re-authenticate. Get credentials from https://developer.x.com/en/portal/dashboard.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.
logout.usage_instructions: Remove stored X API OAuth credentials from the local machine.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

profiles.usage_instructions: Run `x auth profiles --help` for exact arguments and options.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Run `x auth profiles create --help` for exact arguments and options.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Run `x auth profiles delete --help` for exact arguments and options.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.
get.usage_instructions: Run `x auth profiles get --help` for exact arguments and options.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.
list.usage_instructions: Run `x auth profiles list --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

status.usage_instructions: Verify that X API credentials are configured and valid. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Run `x auth test --help` for exact arguments and options.

cache.usage_instructions: Run `x cache --help` for exact arguments and options.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Run `x cache clear --help` for exact arguments and options.

tweet.usage_instructions: CRUD operations on tweets. Use post to create a tweet, list to see your timeline, get to retrieve a specific tweet by ID, and delete to remove one. The X API limits list to 5-100 tweets; filtering is client-side.

delete.usage_instructions: Delete a tweet by its ID. TWEET_ID is a required positional argument.

arguments.name: tweet_id
arguments.help: The ID of the tweet to delete

get.usage_instructions: Retrieve a specific tweet by its ID. TWEET_ID is a required positional argument. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

arguments.name: tweet_id
arguments.help: The ID of the tweet to retrieve

list.help: List tweets from the authenticated user's timeline. The X API does not support server-side filtering, so --filter applies client-side filtering after fetching tweets from the API.
list.usage_instructions: List tweets from your timeline. Use --limit/-l to control count (5-100, default 100). Use --filter/-f for client-side filtering (e.g., "text:contains:hello"). Use --properties/-p to select specific fields (supports dot-notation). Use --table/-t for formatted output. Note: filtering is applied client-side after fetching from the API.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of tweets to return (5-100)

post.help: Post a new tweet to X.
examples: x tweet post "This is a reply" --reply-to 1234567890
post.usage_instructions: Post a tweet. TEXT is a required positional argument (max 280 chars). Use --reply-to/-r to reply to a tweet by ID. Use --quote/-q to quote-tweet by ID.

arguments.name: text
arguments.help: The text content of the tweet (max 280 chars)

usage_instructions: Use for posting and managing tweets on X (formerly Twitter) via the X API v2. Commands follow `x <group> <action>` pattern. Uses OAuth 1.0a authentication. The tweet group handles posting, listing, getting, and deleting tweets.
